stem: A loan application enters the loan processing pipeline. The probability of passing application review is between 72% and 82% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing credit check is 78%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing underwriting is 85%. What is the overall probability that the loan application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.477 to 0.544 as the application review pass rate varies from 72% to 82%. Point estimate: 0.511.

solution_steps: The loan application must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the application review stage creates a 6.7 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the application review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
